questions.stem: Which of the following is a rational number?
questions.explanation: 0.333... = 1/3, which is a ratio of two integers — rational. The others are irrational.

questions.stem: Which statement BEST defines an irrational number?
options: A. A number that cannot be written as a fraction
B. A number that cannot be expressed as a ratio of two integers and has a non-terminating, non-repeating decimal expansion
D. A number with any infinite decimal expansion
questions.explanation: Irrational numbers have non-terminating, non-repeating decimals and cannot be expressed as p/q where p, q are integers and q ≠ 0.

questions.stem: Which of the following is irrational?
questions.explanation: √7 ≈ 2.6457... — non-terminating, non-repeating. The others simplify to rational numbers.

questions.stem: Which set correctly classifies all three numbers: -7, √9, and π?
options: C. -7 and √9 are rational; π is irrational
D. -7 is rational; √9 and π are irrational
questions.explanation: −7 = −7/1 (rational); √9 = 3 (rational); π ≈ 3.14159... (irrational).

questions.stem: Which equation has NO solution?
options: D. x + 2 = 2 + x — wait, re-read options
questions.explanation: 2x + 3 = 2x + 5 simplifies to 3 = 5, a false statement — no solution.

questions.stem: Which equation has INFINITELY MANY solutions?
questions.explanation: 3(x + 2) = 3x + 6 simplifies to 3x + 6 = 3x + 6, a true statement for all x — infinitely many solutions.

questions.stem: On a graph of a system of two linear equations, if the lines are parallel and distinct, the system has:
questions.explanation: Parallel lines never intersect, so there is no point that satisfies both equations.

questions.stem: If the graph of a system shows two lines that are identical (overlapping), the system has:
questions.explanation: Coincident lines share every point — infinitely many solutions.

questions.stem: Given the system: y = 3x + 2 and y = 3x − 4. What type of solution does this system have?
questions.explanation: Same slope (3), different y-intercepts — parallel lines — no solution.

questions.stem: Which set of ordered pairs represents a function?
questions.explanation: In B, each input (3, 4, 5) maps to exactly one output. The same output (5) is allowed; the same input with different outputs is not.

questions.stem: Which statement BEST describes why a vertical line test determines if a graph represents a function?
options: A. A vertical line can only cross a function once because functions have unique outputs for each input
B. Functions must be straight lines
C. Vertical lines test for slope
D. A function must pass through the origin
questions.explanation: If a vertical line crosses the graph more than once, one x-value maps to multiple y-values — not a function.

questions.stem: Which of the following BEST describes a linear function?
options: A. A function whose graph curves
B. A function with a constant rate of change represented by a straight line
C. A function that always passes through the origin
D. A function with a positive slope
questions.explanation: Linear functions have a constant rate of change (slope) and produce a straight-line graph.

questions.stem: Function A is represented by the equation y = 2x + 1. Function B is represented by the table: x: 0,1,2,3 | y: 5,8,11,14. Which function has a greater rate of change?
options: C. They have equal rates of change
questions.explanation: Function B: (14−5)/(3−0) = 9/3 = 3, which is greater than Function A's slope of 2.

questions.stem: A linear model for a ride-share trip is C = 0.85m + 3.50 where C is cost and m is miles. What does the value 3.50 represent?
options: B. The total cost of the trip
C. The flat fee charged at the start of every trip
D. The number of miles traveled
questions.explanation: The y-intercept (3.50) is the value of C when m = 0 — the base/flat fee before any miles are traveled.

questions.stem: Using the same model C = 0.85m + 3.50, what does the value 0.85 represent?
options: C. The additional cost charged for each mile driven
questions.explanation: The slope (0.85) is the rate of change — cost increases by $0.85 for each additional mile.

questions.stem: A graph shows a person's distance from home over time. The graph starts at 0, increases steeply, levels off, then decreases back to 0. Which qualitative description BEST matches?
options: A. The person walks fast, stops, then returns home
B. The person stays home the whole time
C. The person walks at a constant pace with no stops
D. The person's speed increases continuously
questions.explanation: Steep increase = fast walking away, flat section = stopped, decrease back to 0 = returning home.

questions.stem: Which of the following is NOT preserved under a rigid transformation?
options: D. Size of the entire figure after dilation
questions.explanation: Rigid transformations (translation, reflection, rotation) preserve distance and angle measures. Dilation changes size — but dilation is NOT a rigid transformation.

questions.stem: Which statement BEST defines congruent figures?
options: A. Figures that have the same shape but different sizes
B. Figures that can be mapped onto each other using only dilations
C. Figures that can be mapped onto each other by a sequence of rigid transformations
D. Figures that have the same area
questions.explanation: Congruent figures have identical size and shape; one can be mapped to the other through translations, reflections, and/or rotations.

questions.stem: A dilation with scale factor k = 0.5 centered at the origin will:
options: B. Reduce the figure to half its original size
C. Reflect the figure over the x-axis
questions.explanation: Scale factor k < 1 shrinks the figure; k = 0.5 reduces each coordinate to half.

questions.stem: Which sequence of transformations would prove two figures are SIMILAR (but NOT necessarily congruent)?
options: B. A translation followed by a rotation
C. A dilation followed by a reflection
D. A translation followed by another translation
questions.explanation: Similarity requires a dilation (plus any rigid motions). Rigid motions alone produce congruence, not merely similarity.

questions.stem: In a right triangle, the side opposite the right angle is called the:
questions.explanation: The hypotenuse is always opposite the right angle and is the longest side of a right triangle.

questions.stem: Which formula correctly gives the volume of a cylinder with radius r and height h?
questions.explanation: Volume of a cylinder = base area × height = πr² × h.

questions.stem: A cone and a cylinder have the same base radius and height. How does the volume of the cone compare to the volume of the cylinder?
options: A. The cone has the same volume
B. The cone has twice the volume
C. The cone has one-third the volume
D. The cone has half the volume
questions.explanation: V_cone = (1/3)πr²h = (1/3) × V_cylinder

questions.stem: Which formula gives the volume of a sphere with radius r?
questions.explanation: Volume of a sphere = (4/3)πr³

questions.stem: A scatter plot shows that as study time increases, test scores also increase. This is an example of a:
questions.explanation: When both variables increase together, the association is positive.

questions.stem: A line of best fit is drawn on a scatter plot. Which statement BEST describes what this line represents?
options: A. A line connecting all the data points
B. A line that models the general trend of the data
C. A line that only goes through the highest and lowest data points
D. A line with a slope of exactly 1
questions.explanation: A line of best fit (trend line) captures the general direction and magnitude of the data's linear trend.

questions.stem: A model predicts test score (y) from study hours (x): y = 8x + 45. What does the y-intercept 45 represent?
options: A. Score improves by 45 points per hour
B. A student who studies 0 hours is predicted to score 45
D. Average score across all students
questions.explanation: The y-intercept is the predicted value of y when x = 0 — the baseline score with no study time.
